w:r.w:t: Basic knowledge of combinatorics is desirable.
, derivation and Integration. Sets and Venn Diagrams.
(homework, quizzes, midterms, final, programming assignments, lab work, etc.)
Displayed outside office or available on request via e-mail

All the parties involved in first cheating case will be awarded Zero for that evaluation. Afterwards, if any cheating made by any person of the class, will get F in course for all the accused parties.

This course introduces Statistics with emphasis on Probability with application. Topics include variables and their types, pattern of variations, randomness of variables, Combinatorics, probability distributions, Bayesian inference, and linear regression.
The students should achieve a good command of Exploratory data analytical methods and decision-making tools.
Statistics; A Step-by-Step Approach by Allan G. Bluman, 8th Edition
Probability and Statistics for Engineers and Scientists by Walpole.
Advanced Level STATISTICS 1 by Steve Dobbs and Jane Miller.
Introductory Statistics by Barbara Illowsky, De Anza College, Susan Dean, De Anza.
Topics Covered in the Course, with Number of Lectures on Each Topic
Programming Assignments Done in the Course
Different topics are given to the students related to different fields of
. Presentation is expected at the end of the term on these assigned topics which enhances oral and written skills of students.
the basic concept of Statistics and Probability
estimation procedure and analyze statistical test using Z-test and t-test for interpretation.
basic statistical techniques such as regression
and data fitting to engineering data
Definitions of Statistics, Probability, and Key Terms, Data, Sampling, and Variation in Data and Sampling.
Frequency, Frequency Tables, and Levels of Measurement.
Experimental Design and Ethics, Data Collection Experiment, Sampling Experiment.
Histograms, Frequency curves and Cumulative frequency graphs.
Measures of the Location of the Data
Measures of the Center of the Data
Skewness and the Mean, Median, and Mode
Measures of the Spread of the Data, Descriptive Statistics [comparing two data sets using Z].
Basic Rules of Probability, Venn Diagrams.
Introduction to discrete random variables. Proper
Probability Distribution Function (PDF) for a Discrete Random Variable.
Mean or Expected Value and Standard Deviation
Continuous Random Variable and its probability density function.
The Standard Normal Distribution, Using the Normal Distribution,
Using Z table to solve probability problems.
More Questions on practical application of Normal probability Distribution.
Estimation, Error Bound for Mean, Confidence Interval Estimation for Mean using Z Statistics.
Confidence Interval Estimation for Mean using t Statistics.
Error Bound and Sample mean calculations using Confidence Limits.
More Practice Questions on Interval Estimation for Population Mean.
Hypothesis testing, Application of Z test.
Introducing t statistics and t table.
Practice Questions on T and Z tests.
Linear Regression and Correlation, bi-variation Scatter Plot.
Covariance and its interpretation along importance of its sign.
Pearson Correlation coefficient and its interpretation.
Coefficient of determination and its interpretation.
